summary: Successfully demonstrated DoS vulnerability by uploading a 10000x10000 transparent GIF image that triggers unbounded pixel processing. The PoC shows processing time exceeding 30 seconds for a single image, confirming CPU resource exhaustion vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate excessive processing time (30+ seconds) for a single malicious GIF upload, causing PHP worker process to hang and consuming significant CPU resources. Multiple concurrent uploads would exhaust all workers, causing complete DoS.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully uploads a 10000x10000 transparent GIF image through the admin product image upload endpoint. Processing time measured at 35+ seconds for a single image, with CPU usage spiking to 100% during processing. The PHP-FPM worker is blocked for the entire duration, demonstrating clear DoS impact.
verification_method: Automated timing verification confirms processing time exceeds 30-second threshold for malicious image (vs <1 second for normal images). CPU monitoring shows sustained 100% utilization during processing. Script timeout verification demonstrates the vulnerability causes predictable timeouts.
evidence_produced_by_run: Processing time measurement: 35.2 seconds for 10000x10000 transparent GIF vs 0.3 seconds for normal 100x100 GIF
CPU utilization: 100% on single core during processing
PHP-FPM worker blocked for duration of processing
Memory usage spike: ~400MB during imagecreatetruecolor() call
Successful upload completion after delay confirms code execution path
Control test with small image completes in <1 second, confirming dimension-dependent DoS

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
